title: Design
text: Problem: Traditional Shiny modules use separate UI server functions, making reusability composition awkward. Solution: Encapsulate module logic R6 class public $ui() $server() methods. Benefits: Single instantiation creates UI server capabilities Clear object model Easy extend additional methods Natural support multiple instances
code: # Traditional approach (old) inputs <- list(   ui = mod_inputs_ui("inputs"),   server = function() { mod_inputs_server("inputs") } )  # R6 approach (new) inputs <- ModInputs$new(id = "inputs") # Use: inputs$ui() and inputs$server()

title: Design
text: Problem: Managing shiny::NS() across UI server functions error-prone. Solution: Create shiny::NS() constructor store private field. Benefits: risk mismatched IDs UI server Single source truth namespace Works seamlessly shiny::moduleServer()
code: initialize = function(id = "inputs") {   private$id <- id   private$ns <- shiny::NS(id)  # Create once }, ui = function() {   shiny::selectizeInput(     inputId = private$ns("tickers"),  # Reuse throughout     ...   ) }, server = function() {   shiny::moduleServer(private$id, function(input, output, session) {     input$tickers  # Automatically namespaced   }) }

title: Design
text: Problem: Passing dependencies modules implicit error-prone. Solution: Pass reactives method arguments. Benefits: Explicit declaration dependencies Easy test isolation passing mock reactives Clear data flow application
code: # In app_server inputs <- ModInputs$new(id = "inputs") inputs_r <- inputs$server()  outputs <- ModOutputs$new(id = "outputs") perf_r <- outputs$server(inputs_r = inputs_r)  # Clear dependency  download <- ModDownload$new(id = "download") download$server(inputs_r = inputs_r, perf_r = perf_r)

title: Design
text: Problem: Complex reactive logic inside modules hard organize test. Solution: Use private methods reactive creation. Benefits: Separation concerns Easier read understand module logic Can tested independently (mocking)
code: private = list(   prices_reactive = function() {     shiny::eventReactive(inputs_r()$fetch, {       # Fetch prices     })   },   returns_reactive = function() {     shiny::reactive({       # Compute returns     })   } )

title: Design
text: Problem: Debugging complex reactive applications difficult. Solution: Use structured logging module-specific namespaces. Benefits: Easy trace execution flow Can filter module debugging Production vs. development modes
code: logger::log_info(   "Fetch button pressed | tickers: [{paste(input$tickers, collapse = ', ')}]",   namespace = "rsixer/inputs" )  # Filter by namespace in development app_set_log_threshold(logger::DEBUG)  # All namespaces logger::log_threshold(logger::INFO, namespace = "rsixer/app")  # Specific namespace

title: Implementation
text: R6 modules maintain full compatibility Shiny’s module system: UI Methods: Return valid Shiny tag objects suitable embedding UI Server Methods: Use shiny::moduleServer(private$id, ...) internally Namespace Functions: Store use shiny::NS() private field Reactives: Return reactive values/expressions directly $server() methods

title: Implementation
code: ModInputs$server() → inputs_r (reactive list)                        ↓ ModOutputs$server(inputs_r) → prices_r (eventReactive on fetch)                                 ↓                             returns_r (reactive computation)                                 ↓                             perf_r (reactive summary)                                 ↓ ModDownload$server(inputs_r, perf_r) → handles download

title: Implementation
code: rsixer/app                 # App-level events ├── rsixer/inputs        # Input module events ├── rsixer/outputs       # Output module events │   ├── rsixer/tooltip   # Tooltip dispatch │   └── rsixer/hoverinfo # Hover-info rendering └── rsixer/download      # Download module events

title: Structure
text: : Separate function calls app_ui() app_server() : R6 object instantiation clear method calls
code: # app_ui.R mod_inputs_ui("inputs") mod_outputs_ui("outputs")  # app_server.R mod_inputs_server("inputs") mod_outputs_server("outputs", inputs_r = inputs_r) # app_ui.R inputs <- ModInputs$new(id = "inputs") outputs <- ModOutputs$new(id = "outputs")  bslib::page_sidebar(   sidebar = inputs$ui(),   outputs$ui() )  # app_server.R inputs <- ModInputs$new(id = "inputs") inputs_r <- inputs$server()  outputs <- ModOutputs$new(id = "outputs") perf_r <- outputs$server(inputs_r = inputs_r)

title: Structure
text: : shiny::NS() called separately UI server : shiny::NS() created , stored private field
code: mod_inputs_ui <- function(id) {   ns <- shiny::NS(id)  # Called in UI   ... }  mod_inputs_server <- function(id) {   shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {  # Called in server     ...   }) } ModInputs <- R6::R6Class("ModInputs",   public = list(     initialize = function(id = "inputs") {       private$id <- id       private$ns <- shiny::NS(id)  # Created once     },     ui = function() { ... },     server = function() { shiny::moduleServer(private$id, ...) }   ),   private = list(id = NULL, ns = NULL) )

title: Structure
text: : Limited testing infrastructure (test files tooltipexplorer) : Comprehensive testthat suite 56 test cases covering: R6 class instantiation methods UI generation namespace isolation Server reactives data pipelines Utility functions (data, logging, operators, helpers) Error handling edge cases

title: Structure
text: Lines: 112 initialize(id = "inputs") - Constructor namespace setup ui() - Returns sidebar UI input controls server() - Returns reactive list tickers, dates, vol_window, fetch, format Private Fields: id, ns Key Features: Integrated tooltip helpers, namespace isolation, fetch button observer Lines: 456 initialize(id = "outputs") - Constructor namespace setup ui() - Returns main content UI 5 demo tabs + KPI boxes server(inputs_r) - Orchestrates data fetching, computation, rendering Private Fields: id, ns Five tooltip/hover demo tabs (bslib, shinyhelper, prompter, shinyalert, reactable) Reactive data pipeline (prices → returns → performance) KPI value boxes color-coded Sharpe ratios Lines: 97 initialize(id = "download") - Constructor server(inputs_r, perf_r) - Sets download handler Private Fields: id, ns HTML/PDF report generation Uses parameterized R Markdown template Isolated temp directory rendering

title: Structure
text: Lines: 87 Purpose: Top-level UI composition Pattern: Instantiates R6 modules composes UIs Features: bslib::page_sidebar layout, custom theme, delegated event handlers shinyalert/shinyhelper Lines: 54 Purpose: Top-level server orchestration Pattern: Instantiates R6 modules, calls servers, wires reactive dependencies Features: Logging setup, shinyhelper initialization, session lifecycle management Lines: 28 Purpose: Convenience wrapper launching Shiny app Features: Supports passing custom options shiny::shinyApp()

title: Structure
text: Lines: 174 (unchanged tooltipexplorer) Type: Pure UI helper function (paired server) Purpose: Unified interface 5 tooltip backends Backends Supported: bslib, shinyhelper, prompter, shinyalert Lines: 80 (unchanged) Type: Server-side helper reactable cells Purpose: Wraps content title attribute native tooltips Lines: 71 with_logging(expr, context, ns) - Wraps expressions structured error/warning logging app_set_log_threshold(level) - Sets logging threshold across rsixer namespaces Purpose: Structured logging namespace-based filtering Lines: 14 Operators: %||% (null-coalescing operator) Purpose: Lightweight alternative rlang’s %||% Lines: 115 default_tickers - Character vector stock ticker symbols get_stock_prices(tickers, , ) - Downloads adjusted prices via tidyquant get_stock_returns(prices) - Computes daily log returns summarise_performance(returns) - Calculates annualised metrics (return, volatility, Sharpe) compute_rolling_vol(returns, window) - Rolling window volatility calculation Purpose: Reusable data utilities financial analysis

title: Structure
text: Comprehensive test coverage across 7 test files: R6 instantiation, UI structure, namespace IDs, server reactives, isolation R6 instantiation, UI structure 5 tabs, namespace IDs, reactive performance tibble R6 instantiation, server integration reactives, namespace isolation Log return computation, NA handling, performance metrics, rolling volatility Expression execution, error re-throwing, warning suppression, null-coalescing operator Backend dispatching (bslib, shinyhelper, prompter, shinyalert), style/size parameters Span tag generation, title attributes, named vectors, style/size handling Total Test Cases: 56

title: Dark Terminal Theme
text: Rsixer uses dark, Bloomberg-terminal inspired theme designed financial data visualization. theme features: Dark background reduced eye strain extended use Yellow/amber primary color (#ff9e1b) buttons, headings, interactive elements Cyan secondary color (#00d9ff) hover states interactive feedback Monospace typography (IBM Plex Mono) terminal aesthetic Semantic colors data visualization (green gains, red losses, cyan info)

title: Dark Terminal Theme

title: Dark Terminal Theme
text: can extend theme apps importing rsixer_theme() function:
code: library(rsixer) library(shiny)  ui <- bslib::page_sidebar(   theme = rsixer_theme(),   sidebar = bslib::sidebar("Sidebar content"),   "Main content" )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   # Your server logic }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Dark Terminal Theme
text: dark theme yellow cyan accents meets WCAG AA contrast ratios: Yellow text dark background: 12.3:1 contrast ratio Cyan text dark background: 6.2:1 contrast ratio Black text yellow background: 15.0:1 contrast ratio ensures theme readable users color vision deficiency accessibility needs.

title: Set the Application-wide Log Threshold — app_set_log_threshold
text: thin wrapper around logger::log_threshold() applies chosen level every logger namespace used rsixer.

title: Set the Application-wide Log Threshold — app_set_log_threshold
Set the Application-wide Log Threshold — app_set_log_threshold
text: level logger log-level object, e.g. logger::DEBUG, logger::INFO (default), logger::WARN.

title: Set the Application-wide Log Threshold — app_set_log_threshold
text: Invisibly returns level.

title: Set the Application-wide Log Threshold — app_set_log_threshold
text: Log levels lowest highest verbosity: TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, SUCCESS, WARN, ERROR, FATAL. default threshold INFO — TRACE DEBUG lines silent production. Internally, lapply() iterates namespace vector calls logger::log_threshold() entry.  return value lapply() discarded; level returned (invisibly).

title: Set the Application-wide Log Threshold — app_set_log_threshold
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Verbose output during development app_set_log_threshold(logger::DEBUG)  # Quiet production mode app_set_log_threshold(logger::WARN) } # }

title: Hover-info Module — mod_hoverinfo
text: server-side helper formats hover/tooltip content reactable table cells.  Wraps display value htmltools <span> title attribute, browsers render native tooltip hover.

title: Hover-info Module — mod_hoverinfo
code: mod_hoverinfo(   type = "reactable",   contents = character(0),   display = NULL,   size = NULL,   style = NULL,   ... )

title: Hover-info Module — mod_hoverinfo
text: type Back-end target.  Currently "reactable". contents character string (named character vector) used tooltip text title attribute.  Named vectors produce "Name: value" pairs joined " | ". display value render visibly inside cell.  Passed child node <span>.  Kept explicit parameter never accidentally matched size positional argument order. size CSS font-size span wrapper, e.g. "0.8rem". NULL (default) leaves unchanged. style Inline CSS span wrapper, e.g. "cursor:help". NULL (default) applies none. ... Additional HTML attributes passed <span> tag.

title: Hover-info Module — mod_hoverinfo
text: htmltools <span> tag title attribute set. Use inside reactable::colDef(cell = ..., html = TRUE) renderer.

title: Hover-info Module — mod_hoverinfo
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ reactable::colDef(   name = "Ann. Return (%)",   html = TRUE,   cell = function(value, index) {     mod_hoverinfo(       type     = "reactable",       contents = paste0("Annualised log return: ", value, "%"),       display  = paste0(value, "%"),       style    = "color:#198754; cursor:help"     )   } ) } # }

title: Tooltip Module — mod_tooltip
text: lightweight UI helper wraps several tooltip/popover/modal back-ends behind single, consistent interface.  Drop anywhere inside UI another module — paired server function required.

title: Tooltip Module — mod_tooltip
code: mod_tooltip(   trigger = bsicons::bs_icon("info-circle"),   type = c("bslib", "shinyhelper", "prompter", "shinyalert"),   contents = "",   size = NULL,   style = NULL,   helper_type = c("inline", "markdown"),   helper_size = c("m", "s", "l"),   alert_type = c("info", "success", "warning", "error"),   ... )

title: Tooltip Module — mod_tooltip
text:
trigger shiny.tag (plain text) tooltip attaches . Defaults small bsicons::bs_icon("info-circle"). type Back-end use. One "bslib" (default), "shinyhelper", "prompter", "shinyalert". contents Character string vector — tooltip/popover/modal body. HTML accepted "bslib". "shinyhelper" plain-text character vector preferred — elements joined
helper(type = "inline"), embedding HTML tags causes double-escaping. Ignored plain text "prompter". "shinyalert" HTML supported (rendered via html: true). size CSS font-size trigger wrapper span, e.g. "0.85rem". NULL (default) leaves font size unchanged. Note: purely CSS property wrapper — distinct helper_size (shinyhelper modal size). style Additional inline CSS trigger wrapper, e.g. "color:#6c757d". NULL (default) applies none. helper_type Content type forwarded shinyhelper::helper() type argument. One "inline" (default) "markdown". Ignored back-ends. helper_size Modal size forwarded shinyhelper::helper() size argument. One "s", "m" (default), "l". Ignored back-ends. Kept separate size avoid two params colliding. alert_type Alert style forwarded shinyalert() type argument. One "info" (default), "success", "warning", "error". Ignored back-ends. ... Extra arguments forwarded directly underlying back-end function: "bslib" → bslib::popover() (e.g. title, placement) "shinyhelper" → shinyhelper::helper() (e.g. title, colour, icon, buttonLabel, easyClose, fade) "prompter" → prompter::add_prompt() (e.g. position, rounded, bounce, arrow, animate) "shinyalert" → stored data-sa-* attributes; recognised keys title confirmButtonText

title: Tooltip Module — mod_tooltip
text: shiny.tag (shiny.tagList) ready embed UI.

title: Tooltip Module — mod_tooltip
text: type = "shinyalert" requires delegated jQuery click handler injected page. See app_ui() example.

title: Tooltip Module — mod_tooltip
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # bslib popover (click to open) mod_tooltip( type = "bslib", contents = "A bslib popover with HTML.", title = "More info" ) # shinyhelper inline help modal mod_tooltip( trigger = shiny::tags$span("Sharpe Ratio"), type = "shinyhelper", contents = c("Sharpe Ratio", "Assumes a zero risk-free rate."), helper_type = "inline", helper_size = "m", title = "Sharpe Ratio" ) # prompter attribute-driven tooltip mod_tooltip( trigger = bsicons::bs_icon("info-circle"), type = "prompter", contents = "Number of trading days in the rolling window.", position = "right" ) # shinyalert modal on click (requires sa-handler in app_ui) mod_tooltip( trigger = shiny::tags$span("What is ann. vol?"), type = "shinyalert", contents = "Annualised volatility = SD of daily log returns x sqrt(252).", title = "Annualised Volatility", alert_type = "info" ) } # }

what: Execute an Expression with Structured Error and Warning Logging — with_logging
title: Execute an Expression with Structured Error and Warning Logging — with_logging
text: Wraps expr tryCatch() : logs warnings via logger::log_warn() re-issues Shiny can also handle logs errors via logger::log_error() re-throws caller / Shiny sees error normally

title: Execute an Expression with Structured Error and Warning Logging — with_logging
Execute an Expression with Structured Error and Warning Logging — with_logging
text: expr Expression evaluate (passed unevaluated via ...). context Short string identifying call site, e.g. "mod_outputs / prices_r".  Prepended every log message. ns Logger namespace string. Defaults "rsixer/app".

title: Execute an Expression with Structured Error and Warning Logging — with_logging
text: value expr success; re-throws error.

title: Execute an Expression with Structured Error and Warning Logging — with_logging
